reasoning: The Lean signature correctly formalizes "a group of order 312 has a normal Sylow p-subgroup for some prime p dividing its order." G is universally quantified as any finite group with card G = 312. The existential `∃ (p : ℕ) (P : Sylow p G), p.Prime ∧ (p ∣ card G) ∧ P.Normal` captures "some prime p dividing |G|" via the explicit conjuncts `p.Prime` and `p ∣ card G`, and "a normal Sylow p-subgroup" via `Sylow p G` together with `P.Normal`. Mathlib's `Sylow p G` is defined as a maximal p-subgroup for any natural p, and `P.Normal` denotes normality of the underlying subgroup, matching the informal meaning. No hypotheses are added or dropped, and the quantifier structure matches.
